text:
Market today: Mixed signals. A Fed voice (Goolsbee) saying inflation is still too high is the real driver — that keeps rate-cut hopes in check and puts pressure on high-multiple tech names like yours. Chip stocks are getting extra scrutiny with Microsoft's spending concerns rippling through the sector.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest alarm — down 17.6% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast, and it has the most room to fall before your $85 stop. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.2% with a TRIM signal — size plus loss on margin is a dangerous combo. SPCX is a brand-new, extremely volatile IPO with almost no trading history; tiny moves can be wild, and margin amplifies every tick against you.

Watch: (1) Any Fed speakers this week — one hawkish surprise and your whole book gets hit. (2) Micron — it's suddenly a sector bellwether; where MU goes, SMH and AVGO tend to follow.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers (PLTR, AVGO) both have action signals — honor them before margin turns a bad month into a blown account.
